LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ABITSCH & ABITSCH LLC, JEFFREY ABITSCH, GEORGE ABITSCH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFFS EDUARDO MENDOZA AND DENNISE MENDOZA AND AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANTS K-DUBB SECURITY LLC AND KEVIN M. WHITFIELD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT UNDER BARNES V. BLACK (1999) 71 CAL.APP.4TH 1473, THE FACT THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED OFF THE PREMISES IS NOT DISPOSITIVE OF WHETHER A LANDLORD IS LIABLE. UNDER BARNES THE SCOPE OF A LANDLORD'S DUTY IS DETERMINED BY ROWLAND FACTORS. IN LOOKING AT THE RECORD BEFORE THE COURT IT IS CLEAR THAT THERE ARE SEVERAL TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT GOING TO THE ROWLAND FACTORS. AS SUCH, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO THE APPLEBAUM DEPOSITION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANTS HAVE FAILED TO SHOW THEIR OWN DUE DILIGENCE IN SECURING DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF APPLEBAUM PRIOR TO HIS DEATH. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ABITSCH & ABITSCH LLC, JEFFREY ABITSCH, GEORGE ABITSCH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AGAINST PLAINTIFFS EDUARDO MENDOZA AND DENNISE MENDOZA AND AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANTS K-DUBB SECURITY LLC AND KEVIN M. WHITFIELD.
Appearances: Terence Mayo of Mayo & Mayo (397.1515) for defendants and moving parties; Pamela Bumatay of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich (650.989.5400) for defendant K-Dubb Security; David Anderson (no card provided) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants motion for summary judgment is denied. The court finds that under Barnes v. Black (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1473, the fact that the injury occurred off the premises is not dispositive of whether a landlord is liable. Under Barnes the scope of a landlord's duty is determined by Rowland factors. In looking at the record before the court it is clear that there are several triable issues of fact going to the Rowland factors. As such, defendants have not met their burden. Defendant's objection to the Applebaum deposition is overruled. Defendants have failed to show their own due diligence in securing deposition testimony of Applebaum prior to his death. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
